questions.prompt: Which cyst is most characteristically associated with a non-vital tooth?

options.text: Dentigerous cyst around an impacted crown
Radicular (periapical) cyst at a necrotic apex

questions.prompt: A developmental cyst enveloping the crown of an unerupted tooth is best described as:

options.text: Primordial cyst with no tooth association

questions.prompt: Compared with many radicular cysts, keratocystic odontogenic lesions are notable for:

options.text: Always resolving after pulp therapy alone
Higher recurrence risk and aggressive behavior in many cases

questions.prompt: Inflammatory stimulation from a non-vital pulp most directly contributes to expansion of:

options.text: Radicular cyst formation at the involved apex
Dentigerous cyst around a vital impacted tooth
